states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Waiting for user credentials after connection establishment or reinitialization.

states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: Username provided, waiting for password (PASS command).

states_list.name: AUTHENTICATED
states_list.description: Successful login. Session is active for navigation and metadata commands.

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters established (Passive/Active), awaiting transfer trigger.

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER
states_list.description: File or directory listing transfer in progress.

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: RNFR accepted, awaiting the destination name.

invariants_list.rule: PASS must be sent immediately following a USER command.
The client must be authenticated (USER/PASS sequence completed) before issuing LIST, RETR, STOR, CWD, or PWD.
A PASV or PORT command must be issued prior to any command that initiates a data transfer (LIST, NLST, RETR, STOR).
QUIT terminates the control connection and may be sent at any time.
RNFR must precede RNTO to complete a file rename operation.
If PASS fails (530), the client may restart the authentication sequence with USER.
USER.username: Must be a valid username string
PASS.password: Must be a valid password string
PORT.address_info: Six comma-separated integers (h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2) representing IP and port
TYPE.type_code: Usually 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image (Binary)

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, CAPI, PRUE, SMNT

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.rationale: Response code 331 indicates the server is waiting for the password.
Response code 230 confirms successful login.
Response code 200 confirms the PORT command was accepted.
Response code 150 indicates the data connection is opening for listing.
Standard sequence after setting up passive or active mode.
Response code 150 indicates retrieval is starting.
Response code 150 indicates upload is starting.
Response code 221 terminates the session.
Directory query that does not change session state.
Directory change that does not change session state.
Directory change that does not change session state.
Extended CWD (RFC 775) functionality.
Extended PWD (RFC 775) functionality.
Extended CDUP (RFC 775) functionality.
Directory creation that does not change session state.
Extended MKD (RFC 775) functionality.
Directory removal that does not change session state.
Extended RMD (RFC 775) functionality.
File deletion that does not change session state.
Setting transfer type does not change authenticated status.
Setting transfer mode does not change authenticated status.
Setting structure type does not change authenticated status.
System query does not change session state.
Feature query does not change session state.
Help query does not change session state.
No-operation command designed to maintain state.
Single-file listing command on control channel.
File size query does not change session state.
Status query on control channel.
Interpretation of REIN as entering a resetting phase.
Second step of REIN-initiated re-authentication.
IPv6 equivalent of PORT.
Directory listing command that uses the data channel.
Append command that uses the data channel.
Directory list command initiated after data channel setup.
File append after data channel setup.
Represents returning to the control state after listing completion, though technically triggered by the 226/250 response rather than the command itself.
Represents returning to the control state after listing completion.
Represents returning to the control state after transfer completion.
Represents returning to the control state after transfer completion.

status_changes.description: PASS must be sent immediately following a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP protocol sequence (RFC 959) and directly supported by documentation evidence.

status_changes.description: QUIT terminates the control connection and may be sent at any time.
status_changes.rationale: Universal behavior in FTP; session logs confirm this usage.

status_changes.description: RNFR must precede RNTO to complete a file rename operation.
status_changes.rationale: RNFR identifies the source, RNTO identifies the destination; this sequence is mandatory in the protocol.

status_changes.description: If PASS fails (530), the client may restart the authentication sequence with USER.
status_changes.rationale: Evidence from session 2 shows this exact retry pattern.
Standard directory path parameter for CWD.
Standard file retrieval parameter.
Standard file storage parameter.
